title: Kāla Bhairava Aṣṭākṣara Sādhana
century: Svacchanda Tantra c. 6th–8th c.; Kāla Bhairavāṣṭakam attributed to Śaṅkara c. 8th c.
description: A Śaiva-Bhairava rite addressed to Kāla Bhairava — the "Time-Bhairava" form of Śiva as lord of death and of Kāśī — using an eight-syllable (*aṣṭākṣara*) mantra attested in the Svacchanda Tantra and accompanied by the Kāla Bhairavāṣṭakam hymn attributed to Ādi Śaṅkarācārya. High caution — a Bhairava rite of the Śaiva Mantramārga, traditionally restricted to dīkṣit practitioners. Preserved here as h
